data.narrative:
Near Space Labs, founded by Rema Matevosyan, is a Series B-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $40M+ to date. Valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 5 public-market peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private companies, with 3 same-sector, same-stage private comparables providing direct benchmarking.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $40M raised proxy, implying a $76M valuation. Peer-median anchoring across three Series B space companies—Cowboy Space Corporation ($2.0B), Aalyria ($1.3B), and Hubble Network ($400M)—yields a median of $1.3B, with a distribution range of $400M to $2.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied; while the Space & Aerospace sector trades at a 64.2x median EV/Sales multiple (6 public comps), Near Space Labs' revenue is not disclosed, preventing meaningful application of this method.

Triangulation of the two applicable methods produces a valuation range of $65M (low) to $1.5B (high), with a median of $688M. The substantial spread reflects acute uncertainty about where Near Space Labs falls within its peer cohort—specifically, whether the company's stage, technology differentiation, and capital efficiency align more closely with early-stage or growth-inflected comparables. Revenue trajectory and burn efficiency are critical refinement factors not visible in available data.
